lines.text: Central Perk, everyone is there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, Ross, Mon, is it okay if I bring someone to your parent's anniversary party?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure. Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, who's the guy?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, his name is Parker and I met him at the drycleaners.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oooh, did he put a little starch in your bloomers? (Sits up) Who said that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, he's really great though. He has this incredible zest for life, and he treats me like a queen, except at night when he treats me like the naughty girl I am.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Ross) Oh, by the way. Would it be okay if I gave the toast to mom and dad this year?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, yeah, you sure you want to after what happened at their 20th?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I'd really like to.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, hopefully this time mom won't boo you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes! Every year Ross makes the toast, and it's always really moving, and always makes them cry. Well this year I'm going to make them cry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And you wonder why Ross is their favorite?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! Really! Any time Ross makes a toast everyone cries, and hugs him, and pats him on the back and they all come up to me and say, "God, your brother." Know what they'll say this year? "God, you"

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well I can promise you, at least one person will be crying. (Points to himself) I'm an actor, and any actor worth himself can cry on cue. (snaps fingers)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really you can do that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you kidding me? Watch! (Makes funny faces trying to cry) Well I can't do it with you guys watching me!
Opening Credits
Chandler and Monica's, they're getting ready to leave for the party.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh I'm working on my toast for the party, or as I like to call it. Sob fest 2002. Hey check this out. (Hands him a picture.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's a dog.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's a dead dog. That's Chi-Chi; she died when I was in high school.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's your parents' anniversary and you're going to talk about their dead pet?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The good stuff, huh?
Ross, Joey, and Rachel enter

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You got a present for my parents. That's so sweet.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, in honor of their 35th wedding anniversary, I had a star named after them.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Aww that is so cool.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And I got them a book on Karma Sutra for the elderly.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, do you guys have any extra ribbon?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, sure. What do you need? We got lace, satin, sateen, raffia, gingham, felt, (Pause) and I think my testacles may be in here too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (picking up Chi-Chi's picture) Aww! Chi-Chi! Oh, I loved this dog! Y'know Monica couldn't get braces because Chi-Chi needed knee surgery.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You were the 200-pound 11-year-old who rode her!
Phoebe and Parker enter

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Everybody, this is Parker, Parker this is...

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: No, no, no wait! Don't tell me. Let me guess. (Points as he says their names) Joey, Monica, Ross, Rachel and, I'm sorry Phoebe didn't mention you. (Chandler makes a face) Chandler, I'm kidding all ready you're my favorite!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ha!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Why don't all of you tell me a little about your self?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, actually, I'm sorry we-we probably should get going.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: (laughs) Classic Ross. Rachel, Rachel, oh how you glow. May I? (Puts hand on her stomach)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I, uh, think you already are.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Rachel, you have life growing inside you. Is there anything in this world more miraculous than-Oh a picture of a dog! Whose is this?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's my old dog. He passed away years ago.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Oh well, at least you were lucky to have him. Bow-wow old friend, bow-wow. So where's the party?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's out on the island. It's in Massapequa.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Maaaassapequa, sounds like a magical place. Tell me about Massapequa, is it steep in Native American history? {Transcriber's Note: Interestingly Alec Baldwin was born in Massapequa.}

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, there is an Arby's in the shape of a tee-pee.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I got my note cards. (To Chandler) Do you got the presents?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And I've got the car keys.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: We're driving!?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Aces!
Everyone except Ross and Rachel leave.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So uh, he seems like a nice guy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, yeah I like him a lot.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ya wanna hang back and take our own cab?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, otherwise I'm not going.
The Anniversary Party, Ross and Rachel are arriving and see his parents.

lines.speaker: Mr. and Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Hi

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi! (Kisses his mom.) Hey mom.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: This is such a great party! 35 years. Very impressive, do you guys have any pearls of wisdom?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Jack?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Why would you serve food on such a sharp stick? (Looking a toothpick)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's a good question, dad. That's a good question...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hmmm....

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: (To Ross and Rachel) Congratulations you two!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you...we're so excited

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: And also, congratulations on your wedding.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wha-What?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Can we talk to you for just a y'know... It's just a little thing. Well we think it's absolutely marvelous that you're having this baby out of wedlock, some of our friends are less open-minded. Which is why we've told them all that you're married.

lines.speaker: Ross and Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Thanks for going along with this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dad so what we have to pretend that we're married?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Son, I had to shave my ears for tonight. You can do this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Can you believe that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, if you're going to do the ears, you might as well take a pass at the nosal area.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, us having to lie about being married.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I know I don't either, but ya know what, it's their party, and it's just one night. And we don't even have to lie; we just won't say anything. If it comes up again, we'll just...smile. We'll nod along.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Ross!

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Rachel!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi Aunt Lisa, Uncle Dan

lines.speaker: Aunt Lisa
lines.text: Congratulations on the baby, and on the wedding

lines.speaker: Ross and Rachel
lines.text: Hmmmm....

lines.speaker: Uncle Dan
lines.text: Here's a little something to get you started. (Hands them a check)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Aunt Lisa
lines.text: So, how's married life treating you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looking at the check) Unbelievable!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We love marriage!

lines.speaker: Aunt Lisa
lines.text: Great!
The rest of the gang arrives including Parker.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: What a beautiful place. What a great night! I have to tell you, being here with all of you in Event Room C...I feel so lucky. I think of all the good times that have happened here. The birthdays, the proms, the mitzvahs both bar and bat, but none of them will compare with tonight! My God, I don't want to forget this moment! It's like I want to take a mental picture of you all! Click! (He takes a mental picture of them all.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't think the flash went off.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Dahaaa! (Punches Chandler in the arm and he makes a face of pain.) I'm going to find the men's room, be right back.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'll go with you

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Come on!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Somewhere there is someone with a tranquilizer gun and a huge butterfly net looking for that man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I have to go to the bathroom too, but I don't want him complimenting my thing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm so we weren't in the car! Did he ever let up?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He called the Long Island Expressway a concrete miracle.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (imitating Parker) This room! This night! That waiter! His shoes! I must take a mental picture! (He backs into someone.) Ooh sorry...(He looks behind him then notices its Phoebe then stops his impression.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Were you guys making fun of Parker?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That depends, how much did you hear?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, he a little enthusiastic, what's wrong with that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's just that, it's so much.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, so what I like him! Do I make fun of the people you've dated? Tag, Janice, Mona? No, because friends don't do that. But, do you want my opinion? Do you want it? 'Cause in my opinion, your collective dating record reads like the who's who of human crap. (Walks off)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I feel terrible.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What was wrong with Mona?
Commercial Break
The Anniversary Party, Ross and Rachel have just gotten another wedding present.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Open it! Open it! Open it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah baby!

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: So we never got to hear about your wedding!

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: We were surprise that we weren't invited.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, it was just our parents and 1 or 2 friends. It was a small wedding.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But it was beautiful. I mean it was small, but kind of spectacular.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Where did you have it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: On a cliff, in Barbados, at sunset, and Stevie Wonder sang Isn't She Lovely as I walked down the aisle.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, Stevie's an old family friend. (Hits Ross's chest)

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Oh my God. That sounds amazing. I would love to see pictures.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So would I. You wouldn't think that Annie Liebawitz would forget to put film in the camera.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Would you excuse us for a second? (Pulls Rachel off to the side) Umm.... what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? I'm not you. This may be the only wedding I ever have. I want it to be amazing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, okay. Ooooh, ooh maybe I rode in on a Harley.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, Ross, it has to be realistic.
Cut to Phoebe and Parker

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Are you okay? You seem kind of quiet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I'm fine. I'm great. I'm with you.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: And I'm with you! What a great time to be alive! Look at this plate-bouncy thing. (Bounces the plates) What an inspired solution to man's plate dispensing problems.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hm huh, yeah.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Ah! Oysters! Let me feed you one.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, that's not necessary.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Please.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, actually I don't eat...

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: I won't quit until you try.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, fine! Fine! (Takes the oyster and pretends to eat it while dropping it on the floor) Mmm...hmmmmm....

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: What are they like? I've never had one.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why don't you just try one?

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: No, they look too weird.
Cut to Monica and Chandler

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you doin'?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Just going over my toast. Those two will never know what hit 'em. I can't wait. They're going to be crying so hard. They're going to be fighting for breath.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ya know if you want to, I can just hold them down and you could (Punches the air).
Cut to Rachel and Ross

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And my veil was lace, made by blind, Belgium nuns.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Blind?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, not at first, but it was very intricate work and they said even though they lost their sight, it was all worth it.

lines.speaker: Aunt Lisa
lines.text: I'll bet you looked beautiful...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I don't know about that, but some said that I looked like a floating angel.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: (To Ross) So, how did you propose?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah. That's a great story.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, um, actually, I-I took her to the planetarium. That's-that's where we had our first date. Um, she walked in and I had the room filled with lilies, her favorite flower...

lines.speaker: Aunt Lisa
lines.text: Oh that is so sweet!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Shhh! I want to hear the rest!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Then, Fred Astaire singing The Way You Look Tonight came on the sound system, and the lights came down. And I got down on one knee and written across the dome in the stars were the words "Will you marry me?"
Various oohs and ahhs

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And the ring, was the size of my fist (makes a fist)!
Cut to Phoebe and Joey

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah uh, Phoebe! Look umm, I want to apologize about before, okay? We were being jerks. Parker's a nice guy and I'd like to get to know him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Then you better do it now.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because I'm going to kill him

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What-what?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You guys were right. He's just too excited about...everything. I mean I'm all for living life, but this is the Geller's 35th anniversary. Okay? Let's call a spade a spade this party stinks.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know I'm having the worst time. There was a 15-minute line for the buffet, and when I finally got up to the plates, I slipped on a giant booger!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you sure it wasn't an oyster?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I guess it could've been, I didn't really look at it. Y'know, I just wiped it on Chandler's coat and got the hell out of there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He's just such a great guy I'm so excited about him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh hey, you should be excited about him. There's nothing wrong with him he's a good guy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You think?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Ya know what I think; I think we were all just being too negative.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're right. You're right, he's just embracing life. We could all stand to be a little more like Parker. You know what? I am like him! I'm a sunny, positive person.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Actually, you have a little bit of an edge.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What's that now?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nothing...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh look it's Parker!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Look! It's the bunny hop!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oooh I love it!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: You do?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you kidding? People acting like animals to music. Come on!
Cut to Monica, at the microphone

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay it's time for the toast! Umm now-now, I know that Ross usually gives the toast, but this year I'm going to do it.
Everyone sighs

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, no it's going to be great. Really! Mom, Dad, when I got married, one of the things that made me sure I could do it was the amazing example the two of you set for me. For that and so many other things I want to say thank you. I know I probably don't say it enough, but I love you. (Pretends to cry hoping her parents will join her.) When I look around this room, I'm-I'm saddened by the thought of those who could not be here with us. Nana, my beloved grandmother who would so want to be here, but she can't because she's dead. As is our dog Chi-Chi. I mean look how cute she is. (Holds up the picture and pretends to cry again). Was. (To an old man by the stage.) Do me a favor and pass this to my parents. Remember she's dead. Okay, her and Nana, gone. Wow! Hey does anybody remember when Debra Winger had to say goodbye to her children in Terms of Endearment? (Chandler covers his ears) Didn't see that? No movie fans?! You want to hear something sad? The other day I was watching 60 Minutes these orphans in Romania, who have been so neglected, they were incapable of love. (Waits for people to cry, but doesn't get any tears.) You people are made of stone! Here's to mom and dad! Whatever!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Thank you Monica that was uh, interesting. Wasn't it interesting, Jack?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: (looking at the picture) Why don't I remember this dog?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Ross, why don't you give us your toast now?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, no, Mom, it's just Monica this year.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: You're not going to say anything? On our 35th wedding anniversary

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, of course, Um... Um, everybody? Um, I-I just wanted to say...on behalf of my new bride, Rachel (She turns around and smiles), and myself. Umm, that if...if in 35 years, we're half as happy as you guys are, we'll count ourselves the luckiest people in the world.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: (crying) Oh Ross...

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I just wish Nana were alive to hear Ross's toast.
Phoebe's apartment, Parker and her are entering.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: My God what a fantastically well lit hallway!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Can I get you something to drink? Like a water and Valium?

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: I must say this apartment, its, its, There are no words...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh thank God.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: It's a haven. A third-floor paradise. A modern-day Eden in the midst...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah? I know! I know! Uh huh? Listen why don't we just um, sit and relax? You know just be with each other. Quietly!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: That sounds great. (Sits down) My God this is the most comfortable couch I've ever sat on in my entire life. (Bounces on couch)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Let's try something else, let's play a game.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: I love games!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Shocking! Let's play the game of who can stay quiet the longest. (Giggles)

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Or...Jenga.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But, let's play this one first. And remember whoever talks first loses!
They sit back

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: I lose, now Jenga.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Is something wrong?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wrong? Really? You know the word wrong. Everything isn't perfect? Everything isn't magical? Everything isn't a glow with the light of a million fairies? They were just brake lights, Parker!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Well, excuse me for putting a good spin on a traffic jam!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You don't have to put a good spin on everything.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: I'm sorry that's who I am. I'm a positive person.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! I am a positive person. You are like Santa Clause on Prozac, at Disneyland, getting laid!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: So what do you want me to do, you want me to be more negative, less happy?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Much less happy!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Fine! Well then to quote Ross, "I'd better be going."

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So long! Don't let the best door in the world hit you in the ass on your way out! (He exits and she slams the door behind him.)
There's a knock on door, and Phoebe opens it.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Isn't this the most incredible fight you've ever had in your entire life?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh huh. (Closes door)
Ross and Rachel's, they're returning from the party.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...and then, we could've gone from the ceremony to the reception with you in the sidecar!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, it just wouldn't have been feasible.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But having a dove place the ring on your finger would've been no problem?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It was really fun being married to you tonight.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! And! And, it was the easiest 400 bucks I've ever made.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay Ross, can I uh, can I ask you something?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That proposal, at the planetarium...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know, I know it was stupid.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you kidding?! With the, with the lilies, and-and the song, and the stars! It was...really wonderful! Did you just make that up?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, actually I thought about it when, when we were going out. It's how I imagined I uh, I would ask you to marry me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, that would've been very hard to say no too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's a good thing I didn't do it, because it sounds like it would've been a very expensive wedding. (Rachel laughs) Okay, good night

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Goodnight
They go off to their bedrooms

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Even if the sidecar had a windscreen so your hair wouldn't get messed up?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I will think about it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's all I'm askin'
Ending Credits
Central Perk, Ross and Monica are there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay that's it. I give up. At mom and dad's 40th anniversary, you're the one giving the speech.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know I don't understand why they didn't cry. It was a beautiful speech.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, come on.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! All that stuff you said about true love, you were right, I mean, we did learn a lot from Mom and Dad! And that picture of Chi-Chi with her mischievous grin. And what you said about Nana. Ohh, yeah she really would've wanted to be there. And you know what? I think she was.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (starts to cry) Oh good God, Ross! How the hell do you do it?
End
